message:
feat(signal): port raise_default_signal graceful-shutdown helper (bd-mll8f)

Adds the NetBSD libutil `raise_default_signal(int sig)` helper used
by graceful-shutdown code so a child catching SIGTERM/SIGINT for
cleanup can re-raise it under the default action and have the
parent shell observe the correct WTERMSIG exit status.

Algorithm (matching NetBSD's reference impl):

1. Block `sig` to make the swap-and-raise atomic.
2. Install `SIG_DFL` and remember the previous sigaction.
3. Raise `sig` (kernel queues it because we hold it blocked).
4. Restore the prior signal mask (delivers the queued signal under
   `SIG_DFL`; if the default is term/core the process exits and
   the rest of this function never runs).
5. If we got control back (default was ignore, e.g. SIGURG /
   SIGCHLD), restore the saved handler.

Returns 0 on success, -1 with EINVAL on invalid signal numbers.

Tests: 3 integration tests covering (a) successful round-trip
through SIGURG (SIG_DFL = ignore) verifying the custom handler
isn't invoked under SIG_DFL but IS restored after the call,
(b) out-of-range signal returns -1 EINVAL, (c) signal 0 (the null
signal) is rejected as invalid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-cdqp","title":"ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal test fails (SIGNAL_HIT is 0)","description":"The `ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal` test in `unistd_abi_test.rs` fails because the signal handler installed by `ssignal` is not observing `SIGUSR1` when raised via `gsignal`. The `gsignal` call returns 0 (success), but `SIGNAL_HIT.load()` remains 0 instead of 10. This indicates a bug in the signal trampoline, `sysv_signal`'s translation, or signal delivery latency/blocking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:24:26.488095325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:29:47.257390429Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:29:47.255345646Z","close_reason":"Current tree already fixed: ssignal/gsignal delivery and errno parity tests pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cfm1","title":"Printf DISC-001: Implement banker's rounding (round-half-to-even)","description":"Implement IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) for %.0f and similar precision-limited float formatting. Currently uses round-half-away-from-zero which diverges from glibc.

Reference: sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) should return "2" not "3".
Spec: C11 7.21.6.1 footnote says rounding unspecified but POSIX requires IEEE 754 conformance which specifies round-half-to-even.","acceptance_criteria":"- sprintf("%.0f", 2.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 1.5) returns "2"
- sprintf("%.0f", 3.5) returns "4"
- All printf conformance tests pass including round_half_even
- DISC-001 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:00Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:18:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Changed format_f and format_e in printf.rs to use round_ties_even() instead of round(). Added test_format_float_bankers_rounding test. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","printf"]}
 {"id":"bd-cfm2","title":"Scanf DISC-004: Implement hex float parsing (0x1.fp+2)","description":"Implement C11 hex float format parsing for scanf %a/%A specifiers. Currently hex floats like 0x1.fp+2 (= 7.75) fail to parse.

Format: 0x[h...h][.h...h][pN] where h is hex digit, N is decimal exponent.
Spec: C11 7.21.6.2 paragraph 12 describes %a/%A hex float format.","acceptance_criteria":"- sscanf("0x1.fp+2", "%a", &f) parses correctly to 7.75
- sscanf("0x1.0p10", "%a", &f) parses to 1024.0
- sscanf("0x1p0", "%a", &f) parses to 1.0
- All scanf hex float conformance tests pass
- DISC-004 can be marked FIXED in DISCREPANCIES.md","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:00:01Z","created_by":"claude","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T14:20:00Z","close_reason":"FIXED: Implemented scan_hex_float in scanf.rs to parse hex floats. Added 5 unit tests covering basic, integer, negative, negative exponent, and uppercase cases. Updated DISCREPANCIES.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","discrepancy","ieee754","scanf"]}
-{"id":"bd-cfslu","title":"PORT: strpct/strspct (NetBSD libutil percentage formatters)","description":"Port the NetBSD libutil percentage formatters: char *strpct(char *buf, size_t bufsize, uintmax_t num, uintmax_t denom, size_t precision) and char *strspct(char *buf, size_t bufsize, intmax_t num, intmax_t denom, size_t precision). Each renders 100 * num / denom into buf rounded to `precision` fractional digits, returning buf. denom == 0 yields "0" (or "0.0..0" for precision > 0) per NetBSD convention. strspct preserves the sign of (num * denom). Used by df/iostat/etc for "% Used" columns. Implementation: pure-safe core helper format_percent that does the integer math + rounding without floats; abi shims that snprintf the result into the bounded buffer with truncation guarded. Tests: zero/non-zero precision, denom=0, exact integers, halfway-rounding, signed strspct sign handling, undersized buffer, NULL buf returns NULL.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:10:23.580878704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:10:23.580878704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Each renders 100 * num / denom into buf rounded to `precision` fractional digits, returning buf. denom == 0 yields "0" (or "0.0..0" for precision > 0) per NetBSD convention. strspct preserves the sign of (num * denom). Used by df/iostat/etc for "% Used" columns. Implementation: pure-safe core helper format_percent that does the integer math + rounding without floats; abi shims that snprintf the result into the bounded buffer with truncation guarded. Tests: zero/non-zero precision, denom=0, exact integers, halfway-rounding, signed strspct sign handling, undersized buffer, NULL buf returns NULL.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:10:23.580878704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:16:59.729635103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:16:59.727624285Z","close_reason":"shipped: strpct + strspct percentage formatters with u128 widening + integer rounding + signed/unsigned variants + 11 core tests + 12 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cgodw","title":"fuzz_dlfcn harness: dlsym/dladdr on stale dlclose'd handle passes UB pointer to ld-linux","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dlfcn.rs Op::Sym/Op::SymFuzz/Op::Addr call dlsym on handles returned from pick_slot without checking State::Live. When a prior Close op transitioned the handle to Stale, subsequent Sym ops pass a freed handle into real libc dlsym, which dereferences it inside the dynamic loader and SEGV's. Reproducer: artifacts/fuzz_dlfcn/crash-81b48dad2c4463a9ba111dda2a7debed80d78913. Same shape as bd-2t616 (pidfd_timer), bd-reb0e (TimerDelete), earlier fuzz_socket/mmap stale fixes. Fix: skip op when h.state != State::Live. Harness bug, not a library bug.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:09:59.917913944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:17:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Sym/SymFuzz/Addr and Close now skip stale handles. dlclose on already-closed handle corrupts glibc loader state; manifests as later-SEGV on dlsym of unrelated LIVE handle. 177K runs clean after fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cgqe","title":"pattern: execute regex_glob_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/regex_glob_ops_conformance_test.rs still executes tests/conformance/fixtures/regex_glob_ops.json through the in-process TestRunner path. Replace that coverage with isolated harness subprocess execution, assert expected_output plus host parity for regcomp/regexec/fnmatch/glob/wordexp in strict and hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path mismatches the new executable coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:06:43.126457693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:09:31.175231942Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:09:31.173166789Z","close_reason":"Replaced in-process regex_glob_ops TestRunner coverage with isolated harness subprocess execution and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cie59","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] pthread_cond_wait/timedwait clobbers RECURSIVE/ERRORCHECK mutex owner without ownership check","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs lines 2541-2554 (pthread_cond_wait) and 2775-2788 (pthread_cond_timedwait), for PTHREAD_MUTEX_RECURSIVE_TYPE and PTHREAD_MUTEX_ERRORCHECK_TYPE the code unconditionally clears the mutex owner (owner.store(MUTEX_NO_OWNER)) and zeros the recursive lock count (count.swap(0)), without first verifying that the calling thread is actually the owner.

If thread A holds a recursive mutex with count=N and thread B incorrectly invokes pthread_cond_wait on that same mutex (a POSIX violation, but glibc detects it for ERRORCHECK), the current code:
1. Saves count (which belongs to A) into thread B's stack-local saved_count
2. Stores NO_OWNER, destroying A's ownership record
3. Resets count to 0, destroying A's recursion depth
4. Waits on the condvar
5. On wake, claims ownership for thread B and restores A's saved count to thread B

This effectively allows thread B to silently steal the mutex. POSIX mandates EPERM for pthread_cond_wait with an unlocked or non-owned ERRORCHECK mutex.

Repro: Two threads, mutex_init with PTHREAD_MUTEX_ERRORCHECK; thread A locks; thread B calls pthread_cond_wait without locking; expect EPERM, observe owner state corruption.

Fix: Before saving/clearing owner, read current owner via owner.load(Acquire) and compare to core_self_tid(). If mismatch and type is ERRORCHECK, return EPERM. For RECURSIVE, also return EPERM (POSIX says behavior is undefined, but EPERM is safer than corruption).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:52:46.881702695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: commit 64fb99b2 added owner verification in pthread_cond_wait/timedwait for RECURSIVE/ERRORCHECK; returns EPERM for non-owner. Regression test added.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -912,6 +912,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-mjon","title":"bd-yos subtask: Thread bootstrap trampoline finalization for pthread_create","description":"Background:
- Thread bootstrap is a core blocker for both callthrough removal and TLS key APIs.

Goal:
- Finalize clone/trampoline startup path for pthread_create with explicit invariants and ABI boundaries.

Deliverables:
1) Thread start trampoline contract (entry args, return handling, cleanup path).
2) Parent/child synchronization at startup.
3) Error path behavior and errno propagation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- pthread_create success/failure semantics match declared support matrix behavior.
- No hidden host-libc dependency in startup path for covered mode/profile.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for bootstrap components and error branches.
- Structured logs: trace_id, thread_id, parent_id, start_state, exit_state, errno, timing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonForge","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.822186049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139407950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:27.139340033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":1123,"issue_id":"bd-mjon","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Thread bootstrap trampoline implementation.

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/raw.rs: Added clone_thread_asm (inline asm trampoline for Linux clone syscall)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added SYS_GETTID, SYS_SET_TID_ADDRESS, sys_gettid, sys_exit_thread, sys_clone_thread
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: Complete native thread creation via clone (ThreadHandle, create_thread, join_thread, detach_thread, self_tid)
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/mod.rs: Updated re-exports

Quality gates:
- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-core: PASS
- cargo check -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-core --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::thread: 6/6 PASS

Tests:
- create_and_join_thread_returns_value
- child_thread_can_write_shared_memory
- multiple_threads_created_and_joined
- detach_thread_does_not_error
- join_null_handle_returns_einval
- gettid_returns_positive

Architecture:
- Uses CLONE_VM|CLONE_FS|CLONE_FILES|CLONE_SIGHAND|CLONE_THREAD|CLONE_SYSVSEM|CLONE_SETTLS|CLONE_PARENT_SETTID|CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID
- Child stack: [fn_ptr][arg] layout, trampoline pops both, calls fn_ptr(arg), SYS_exit on return
- Join uses futex-wait on TID (kernel clears via CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID and wakes waiters)
- Parent/child sync via startup futex (ensures args consumed before create returns)
- 2MiB stack with 4KiB guard page via mmap","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:55:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mk07a","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc ctype.h diff matrix","description":"Differential conformance harness shipped in commit 631b5100 (crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/conformance_diff_ctype.rs).

Coverage:
- 12 classifiers (isalpha/isdigit/isalnum/isspace/isupper/islower/isprint/ispunct/isxdigit/isblank/iscntrl/isgraph)
- 2 transformers (toupper/tolower)
- Sweep: EOF + every byte 0..=255 (257 inputs)
- 3598 differential calls vs glibc reference
- 0 divergences observed

Result: ctype.h family is conformant against glibc in the C/POSIX locale.

Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_conformance_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test conformance_diff_ctype","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped in commit 631b5100; 0 divergences against glibc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}
 {"id":"bd-mkf1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc mkfifo+mknod diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 4 functions: mkfifo (S_IFIFO mode round-trip via stat + EEXIST edge), mkfifoat (AT_FDCWD), mknod (S_IFREG + S_IFIFO modes), mknodat (AT_FDCWD). Char/block device creation excluded (CAP_MKNOD required). 7 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T14:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T14:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T14:00:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","mkfifo","mknod"]}
+{"id":"bd-mll8f","title":"PORT: raise_default_signal (NetBSD libutil graceful-shutdown helper)","description":"Port the NetBSD libutil raise_default_signal(sig) helper. It saves the current sigaction for sig, installs SIG_DFL with the same mask cleared, raises the signal (which terminates the process if the default action is core/term), then if control returns restores the prior sigaction and unblocks. Used by graceful-shutdown code paths so that SIGTERM/SIGINT propagate the correct exit status from the parent shell. Signature: int raise_default_signal(int sig). Returns 0 on success, -1 with errno on failure. Implementation: thin abi shim using existing sigaction/sigprocmask wrappers; tests cover SIG_USR1 round trip with a custom handler that flags pre/post invocation, NULL-handler default, error path on bad signum.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:21:05.903676765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:21:05.903676765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mn-1","title":"PORT: mntent sub-bead 1 — core::mntent pure-safe parser/serializer","description":"Add crates/frankenlibc-core/src/mntent/mod.rs with: MntFields<a> (six byte-slice field references), parse_mntent_line(line) -> Option<MntFields> handling blank/comment lines, freq/passno default-zero parsing, has_mnt_opt(opts, opt) -> Option<usize> scanning comma-separated tokens, and format_mntent_line(fields, out: &mut Vec<u8>) producing fstab-style "fsname dir type opts freq passno
". All operate on &[u8]; no allocation in parsers; tests cover blank/comment skip, partial fields, leading/trailing whitespace, options with embedded commas as separators, edge-case option matching at start/end of string.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:27:47.180840Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:29:33.308150Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:29:33.308150Z","close_reason":"Added crates/frankenlibc-core/src/mntent/{mod.rs,parse.rs} with: borrowed MntFields<a> (six byte-slice field references), parse_mntent_line() (handles blank/comment lines, EOL stripping, runs of whitespace, default-zero freq/passno fallback), has_mnt_opt() (token-aware comma-separated option scan), parse_mntent_freq_passno() (i32 with garbage-fallback), and format_mntent_line() (in-place i32-to-decimal serialization including i32::MIN). 26 unit tests cover blank/comment skip, partial fields, leading whitespace, run-collapse, embedded-token boundary cases (substring guard), key=value option matching, round-trip identity, and a real /proc/mounts line.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mn-2","title":"PORT: mntent sub-bead 2 — rewire abi shims to core","description":"Refactor unistd_abi.rs hasmntopt, getmntent_r, addmntent to delegate to core::mntent::{has_mnt_opt, parse_mntent_line, format_mntent_line}. Keep the C-ABI marshalling (mntent struct field pointers, caller buffer packing, BufReader/Write integration) at the boundary. Verify any conformance_diff_mntent tests still pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:27:47.180840Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:31:32.159525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:31:32.159525Z","close_reason":"Refactored frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs hasmntopt (was ~12 lines of inline window/comma scan), getmntent_r (was ~85 lines of inline parser + 4x copy_nonoverlapping NUL packing), and addmntent (was ~15 lines of CStr->String->format!). All three now delegate to frankenlibc_core::mntent::{has_mnt_opt, parse_mntent_line, format_mntent_line}. The C-ABI marshalling — mntent struct field-pointer writes, caller-buffer NUL packing via a closure, BufReader/Write integration — stays at the boundary. Verified: 2/2 mntent unistd_abi tests pass, 178/178 unistd_abi tests pass overall.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mn-epic","title":"PORT: mntent (fstab/mtab) family native Rust impl","description":"Move getmntent_r line-parsing, hasmntopt option scan, and addmntent line formatting from frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (~300 lines of pointer arithmetic + manual NUL packing) into a pure-safe core::mntent module operating on byte slices. Sub-beads: 1) add core::mntent with parse_line + has_opt + format_line + comprehensive byte-slice tests; 2) rewire abi shims to delegate to core, keeping the C ABI struct-layout marshalling at the boundary.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:27:47.180840Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:31:32.159525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T09:31:32.159525Z","close_reason":"Epic complete in 2 sub-beads. bd-mn-1: core::mntent module (parse_mntent_line, has_mnt_opt, format_mntent_line, parse_mntent_freq_passno) with 26 unit tests. bd-mn-2: abi shims rewired. Net effect: ~110 lines of inline parsing + manual NUL packing in unistd_abi.rs replaced by ~50 lines of marshalling over a pure-safe Rust module that lives inside #![deny(unsafe_code)]. The line parser is now reachable from any caller (not just getmntent_r) and exhaustively tested in isolation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
